title: SKILL.md, llms.txt, AGENTS.md, and verification-rule catalog
short_description: Ship the in-repo, version-pinned agent onboarding surface: the authoring-loop SKILL.md, llms.txt, AGENTS.md, and a verification-rule catalog documenting every finding code with fix patterns, all describing the real frozen surface.
long_description_md:
# Agent docs surface

## Why this exists

Product-spec capability 11: agent onboarding surface is a shipped success metric, not an afterthought. Feasibility report evidence: badly structured agent docs make agents *worse*, not neutral. So this surface engineered, tested, not hand-waved. Whole product bet: agent reads docs once, then loops verify/fix/render on its own. Docs are the entry ramp for that loop.

## Roadmap fit

Phase 7, first done-when bullet, verbatim: "SKILL.md (authoring-loop skill), llms.txt, AGENTS.md shipped in-repo; verification-rule catalog documents every finding code with fix patterns." Last phase before launch on purpose — docs describe the real frozen surface, not a moving target. Depends on `taste-constraint-rules` (Phase 5, pacing/continuity codes), `map-verify-rules` (Phase 4, MAP.* codes), `chapter-project-structure` (Phase 6, multi-scene project layout docs describe) — by the time this task runs, every rule family and the long-form project shape exist for real, so nothing documented here is speculative.

Sibling tasks: `cookbook-examples` builds the worked examples this task's docs point at — don't duplicate cookbook content here, link to it. `fresh-agent-test` runs a live agent against this surface — don't run agent sessions here, just ship docs an agent *can* succeed from.

## Required scope

**SKILL.md** (repo root, cross-vendor agent-skills format — YAML frontmatter with `name`/`description`, markdown body). Teaches authoring loop: scaffold (`anima new`) → write scenes (script.md + project.py) → verify (`anima verify --json`) → fix from findings → preview (`anima preview`) → render (`anima render`). Every CLI command named must be a real shipped subcommand — no aspirational commands. Version-pinned: frontmatter or a stamped line ties doc content to `anima.__version__` so a docs/engine drift is a build failure, not a silent lie.

**llms.txt** (repo root, llms.txt convention: H1 project name, blockquote one-line summary, H2 sections with markdown links to docs/cookbook/API reference). Every link must resolve to a real file in-repo.

**AGENTS.md** (repo root): install (`pip install anima-ai`), the authoring loop restated for agents landing without SKILL.md context, CLI contract summary (`--json`, exit codes 0/1/2/3, stdout/stderr separation — pulled from `cli-framework`'s actual contract, never restated loosely), pointer to cookbook and verification-rule catalog.

**Verification-rule catalog** (`docs/verification-rules.md`): one entry per registered `Finding` code in the code registry (`verification-report-schema` + every rule-family task that registered codes into it — `REF.*`, `TIMING.*`, `OVERLAP.*`, `MARGIN.*`, `FONT.*`, `CONTRAST.*`, `MAP.*`, `PACING.*`, `CONTINUITY.*`, `STYLE.*`, `BUILD.EXCEPTION`). Each entry: code, namespace, severity, plain description, and a concrete example `fix_hint` pattern an agent can pattern-match. This is a coverage-tested artifact, not prose written once and left to rot — a new code shipping without a catalog entry is a caught regression, not a doc debt.

## Out of scope

- Cookbook worked examples (map scene, character scene, 2-min episode) — `cookbook-examples` builds those; this task only links to them.
- Running an actual fresh-agent session against this surface — `fresh-agent-test`.
- Public docs site (mkdocs-material, GitHub Pages) — Phase 8 launch scope.
- Any new verification rule, CLI command, or engine behavior — this task documents what already shipped, builds nothing new in the engine.
- Context7 listing submission — Phase 8, external action, flagged separately.
roadmap_phase: Phase 7: Agent onboarding surface + the v1 bar

deliverables.description: SKILL.md at repo root, valid agent-skills frontmatter (name, description), teaches scaffold->write->verify->fix->preview->render loop using only real shipped `anima` subcommands, version-pinned to `anima.__version__`. Test parses frontmatter + body, cross-checks every named `anima <cmd>` against `anima --help --json` output, fails on any TODO/placeholder text.

llms.txt and AGENTS.md at repo root, llms.txt convention structure (H1/blockquote/H2 link sections for llms.txt; install+loop+CLI-contract+pointers for AGENTS.md). Test parses structure and asserts every linked path resolves to a real in-repo file.

docs/verification-rules.md catalog: one entry (code, namespace, severity, description, example fix_hint) per Finding code currently registered in anima's code registry. Coverage test imports the live registry and fails the build if any registered code lacks a catalog entry.

Version-pin integrity check: SKILL.md/llms.txt/AGENTS.md's declared engine-version pin matches the installed `anima.__version__` at test time, so a release without a docs bump fails CI instead of shipping stale-pinned docs.
